subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: rebuilt from independent sources (UN/World Bank/IMF, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, CPJ, UN OCHA, IPC, PAHO, Security Council Report) + independent press.
Data limitation: Haitian official statistics have collapsed and figures rely on UN/INGO estimation in gang-inaccessible areas; confidence is capped accordingly.

Standout drags: state collapse with gang control of the capital, a hyperinflationary economy, a collapsed health system with famine pockets, and catastrophic crime. Among the most negative profiles in the dataset.

economic.notes: A collapsed, aid-dependent, hyperinflationary economy with a gang-strangled capital.

fiscal_state.notes: Aid-dependent, in multi-year contraction, with no functioning tax base and a gang-paralyzed capital economy. (World Bank/IMF; OCHA)

monetary_independence.notes: A barely-functional central bank, partly dollarized, with no real policy transmission. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The gourde is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Around 28% with a hyperinflationary multi-year cumulative. (IMF WEO)

banking_stability.notes: Functioning only in shrinking safe zones, gang-disrupted with broken cash logistics. (press; OCHA)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Collapsed apparel/remittance economy with the capital's port gang-strangled and routes blockaded. (Security Council Report; UNODC)

institutional.notes: State collapse - no elected government, gangs governing the capital, collapsed data, journalists killed.

rule_of_law.notes: No elected government; gangs control ~85%+ of the capital (taxing, judging), with a non-functional judiciary. (NPR; UN)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Hollow - only ~40% of health facilities operational illustrates the state's reach. (PAHO)

press_freedom.notes: A poor RSF standing, with journalists killed by gangs and operating in exile under impunity. (RSF; CPJ)

political_social.notes: No elected government, gang rule of the capital, mass displacement, and mass sexual violence.

political_stability.notes: No president since the 2021 assassination; a transitional council with a lapsed mandate; gangs rule the capital and a UN-backed mission struggles. (CSIS; Security Council Report)

civil_liberties.notes: Collapsed under gang rule, with mass sexual violence and movement restrictions. (HRW; OCHA)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A mass-emigration state, not a destination. (inferred)

social_cohesion.notes: Gang-fractured, with ~1.4M internally displaced (~12% of the population). (OCHA)

demographics.notes: Young but catastrophe-stricken, with brain-drain emigration. (UN)

geopolitical.notes: No sovereign capacity, dependent on a UN-backed security mission, in a gang-warfare collapse.

alliance_reliability.notes: No sovereign capacity; reliant on a UN-backed mission after the Kenya-led MSS wound down, with recurring transition failures. (Security Council Report)

conflict_involvement.notes: A quasi-civil gang war, with thousands killed in 2025. (Security Council Report; UNODC)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: A UN arms embargo and targeted gang sanctions, with limited enforcement - isolated. (UN News)

physical_practical.notes: Extreme disaster exposure, a collapsed health system with famine pockets, and catastrophic crime.

climate.notes: The lowest-readiness state in the hemisphere - deforestation plus hurricanes plus drought plus collapsed governance. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: Collapsed, with cholera resurgence and IPC-5 famine pockets; only a fraction of capital hospitals operating. (PAHO; IPC)

infrastructure.notes: Gang-controlled routes and ports, with storm damage. (Security Council Report; OCHA)

crime_safety.notes: Catastrophic - gang control, kidnapping, and massacres, among the world's most dangerous. (UNODC; ACLED-cited press)

summary: A state in collapse: no elected government since the 2021 presidential assassination, gangs controlling roughly 85% of the capital (taxing and 'judging'), and a UN-backed security mission struggling to hold. The economy is hyperinflationary and aid-dependent with the capital's port strangled; the health system has collapsed amid cholera and famine pockets; and crime is catastrophic, with mass displacement (~1.4M) and sexual violence. Figures rely on UN/INGO estimation in gang-inaccessible areas; the profile is among the most negative in the dataset.
